According to a Harvard study, married people are found 20% 19 likely to suffer from cancer, compared with people who are single, divorced, or widowed. This study, conducted on more than 730,000 people, 20 in one of the latest medical journals. These married people are more likely to discover their diseases 21 an earlier stage, and also to get proper treatments. “Our data 22 that marriage can have a significant health impact for patients with cancer,” says Ayal Aizer, a doctor of Harvard Radiation Oncology Program. 23, the researchers do not see their study as an affirmation of marriage, but rather it should send a message to anyone who has a friend or a loved one with cancer: being there for that person, and 24 support. The result of the research indicates 25 a spouse or a close partner can push the patients to seek medical help and support them during grueling chemotherapy, radiation and other treatments.

options: in vain 四、閱讀測驗 ( 第 33-40 題,每題 2 分,共 16 分 ) 說明:以下有兩篇短文,共有 8 個題目,為第 33 至 40 題,請於閱讀短文後,選出最適當的答案 。 ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 33-36 題。 Michael Alfred was assigned a task in the Tsavo National Park in northern Kenya for a film. He and his colleagues set off early and their destination was a remote waterhole. There they saw a magnificent bull elephant, covered with dry mud, stepped forward to drink. This creature drank calmly and deeply; it might have traveled 50 kilometers to reach the water. It was not going to hurry, and as it drank, it rested in the shade. Yet, this bull faltered briefly and then suddenly collapsed. It was dead within minutes. Michael approached this giant creature and removed the head of a poisoned arrow from its side. Shocked after seeing this, Michael pressed the shutter button. It was utterly shocking to learn that there are so many different ways to kill elephants in Africa in order to hunt for the tusks. In the last year alone, elephants in Africa have been targeted with automatic rifles, poisonous arrows, and contaminated water. Many elephants recently suffered from poisonous arrows, causing a slow and lingering death. Michael and his coworkers were sad to know this, but they had to spend a month at the waterhole, filming the herds as they came to drink and went somewhere.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: This passage focuses on the topic of.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: What did Michael do after he pulled out the head of the poisonous arrow?
options: He pressed on the wound of the elephant.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: What is NOT mentioned as the ways to kill an elephant?

questions.q: What does collapsed mean in this passage?
options: slept away ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 37-40 題。 The Internet has become the revolutionary source of information. Tens of thousands of messages flood in the cyber world with just a click. Let’s think of the questions: Why are those people so willing to share information on the Internet? Actually, there are all kinds of variations. Some people put up misinformation because they are misinformed, while some might do that on purpose since there is no one responsible for the content, and no institution accounting for its accuracy. Internet misinformation is never a small thing. The World Economic Forum (WEF) in 2013 identified this issue as the top ten world crisis, accompanied by rising societal tensions in the Middle East and North Africa, widening income disparities, persistent structural unemployment, and inaction on climate change. With a value of 3.35 on a scale of 1-5, this issue is seen as “somewhat to very significant.” However, how can people fight such misinformation? To start with, people should stop forwarding email without confirming the authenticity. Do not easily distribute the information from strangers to our friends. In addition, always get the information from the respectable news sites and government sites. Meanwhile, it becomes increasingly important to teach the kids how to think critically about what they are seeing. It is not simply on the Internet; television, newspapers, radio, or even our friends are possibly delivering misinformation. People in the modern era have to learn to think about what they see and what they hear in order to discern what they should pay attention to and what they should not.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: The World Economic Forum lists Internet misinformation as an important problem around the world.
Advanced technology nowadays has brought many advantages and also some disadvantages to our life.
False and inaccurate information on the Internet has become a critical problem and should be carefully taken care of.
People intentionally distribute the misinformation because they would like to spread vicious virus.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: Which of the followings is NOT a good idea to fight Internet misinformation, according to the passage?
options: We should be capable of thinking critically.
We need to check the source before forwarding the email.
We are expected to install anti-virus program to ensure our data is secure and safe.
We might want to retrieve information from respectable sites.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.q: What is also listed as the top ten world problem in the World Economic Forum?
options: International Trade in Middle East and Northern Africa
Passivity in dealing with weather problem
